preamble: Diphthongization of the nasalized mid-back vowel õ into the rising diphthong uo, often found in Romance and Germanic historical stages.

examples.note: Breaking of nasalized mid vowels in various dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
